𝗣𝗢𝗦𝗦𝗜𝗕𝗟𝗘 𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗦𝗜𝗗𝗘𝗥𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗦
• A prolonged fever can be a sign of an underlying infection or illness that needs medical attention.
• Possible considerations include pneumonia, influenza, or other viral or bacterial infections.

• Do not attempt to treat your fever with home remedies; instead, seek medical attention.

• If you experience any emergency warning signs such as difficulty breathing, chest pain, or severe headache, seek immediate attention.
• Remember, this is not a medical diagnosis. Please consult a healthcare professional.

📋 𝗖𝗢𝗠𝗣𝗥𝗘𝗛𝗘𝗡𝗦𝗜𝗩𝗘 𝗛𝗘𝗔𝗟𝗧𝗛 𝗔𝗦𝗦𝗘𝗦𝗦𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧
========================================================

📊 𝗦𝗬𝗠𝗣𝗧𝗢𝗠 𝗦𝗨𝗠𝗠𝗔𝗥𝗬
• Fever: 5 days, severity unknown
• No other symptoms reported
• No additional information on duration or severity of symptoms

🧬 𝗙𝗔𝗠𝗜𝗟𝗬 𝗛𝗜𝗦𝗧𝗢𝗥𝗬 𝗔𝗡𝗔𝗟𝗬𝗦𝗜𝗦
• No family history provided
• No genetic risk implications available

🏥 𝗣𝗢𝗦𝗦𝗜𝗕𝗟𝗘 𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗗𝗜𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗦 (𝗹𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝗺𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝘁𝗼 𝗹𝗲𝗮𝘀𝘁 𝗹𝗶𝗸𝗲𝗹𝘆)
1. Influenza
2. Pneumonia
3. Viral or bacterial infections

📊 𝗥𝗜𝗦𝗞 𝗔𝗦𝗦𝗘𝗦𝗦𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧
• Risk Level: HIGH
• Risk Score: 80/100
• Key Risk Factors:
  - Prolonged fever (5 days)
  - Unknown severity of symptoms
  - Potential for underlying infection or illness

💊 𝗥𝗘𝗖𝗢𝗠𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗗𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗦
1. Consult a healthcare professional for a thorough evaluation and guidance
2. Provide a detailed medical history, including any recent travel or exposure to sick individuals
3. Discuss potential treatment options, such as antiviral or antibiotic medications
4. Follow the healthcare professional's recommendations for further testing or treatment

🚨 𝗪𝗛𝗘𝗡 𝗧𝗢 𝗦𝗘𝗘𝗞 𝗜𝗠𝗠𝗘𝗗𝗜𝗔𝗧𝗘 𝗛𝗘𝗟𝗣
• Difficulty breathing
• Chest pain
• Severe headache

📋 𝗡𝗘𝗫𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗘𝗣𝗦
• Schedule an appointment with a healthcare professional as soon as possible
• Be prepared to provide detailed information about symptoms and medical history
• Follow the healthcare professional's recommendations for treatment and follow-up care

This is not a medical diagnosis. Consult a healthcare professional.

symptoms.message: I have fever, cough, and severe headache for 3 days.

conversation.content: I have fever, cough, and severe headache for 3 days.

📋 𝗦𝗬𝗠𝗣𝗧𝗢𝗠 𝗔𝗖𝗞𝗡𝗢𝗪𝗟𝗘𝗗𝗚𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧
• I notice you're experiencing: fever, cough, headache
• Duration: 3 days


📋 𝗙𝗢𝗟𝗟𝗢𝗪-𝗨𝗣 𝗤𝗨𝗘𝗦𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗦
• Can you tell me more about when these symptoms started?
• Have you taken any medication for these symptoms?
• Do you have any other symptoms not mentioned?

📋 𝗥𝗘𝗖𝗢𝗠𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗗𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗦
• Consider scheduling a doctor consultation
• Monitor your symptoms and track any changes
• Stay hydrated and get adequate rest

📋 𝗪𝗛𝗘𝗡 𝗧𝗢 𝗦𝗘𝗘𝗞 𝗛𝗘𝗟𝗣
• If symptoms worsen or persist
• If you experience severe pain or difficulty breathing

• Risk Level: Moderate
• Risk Score: 45/100

💊 𝗥𝗘𝗖𝗢𝗠𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗗𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗦
